metadata.note: Token counts are accurate regardless of mode. Simulated mode uses pre-crafted realistic conversations.

test1_a2a_communication.name: Create Stripe Charge
test1_a2a_communication.task: Create a Stripe charge for $50 to customer cus_123

conversation_log.content:
I need you to create a Stripe charge for $50 to customer cus_123. Here is the Stripe API documentation you'll need:

# Stripe API - Charges

## POST /v1/charges

Creates a new charge object. To charge a credit card or other payment source, you create a Charge object.

### Parameters

- **amount** (required) — A positive integer representing how much to charge in the smallest currency unit (e.g., 100 cents to charge $1.00). The minimum amount is $0.50 US or equivalent in charge currency. The amount value supports up to eight digits (e.g., a value of 99999999 for a USD charge of $999,999.99).
- **currency** (required) — Three-letter ISO currency code, in lowercase. Must be a supported currency.
- **customer** (optional) — The ID of an existing customer that will be charged in this request.
- **source** (optional) — A payment source to be charged. This can be the ID of a card (i.e., credit or debit card), a bank account, a source, a token, or a connected account.
- **description** (optional) — An arbitrary string which you can attach to a Charge object. It is displayed when in the web interface alongside the charge.
- **metadata** (optional) — Set of key-value pairs that you can attach to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format.
- **receipt_email** (optional) — The email address to which this charge's receipt will be sent.
- **shipping** (optional) — Shipping information for the charge. Helps prevent fraud on charges for physical goods.
- **statement_descriptor** (optional) — For card charges, use statement_descriptor_suffix. Otherwise, you can use this value as the complete description of a charge on your customers' statements.
- **capture** (optional) — Whether to immediately capture the charge. Defaults to true. When false, the charge issues an authorization (or pre-authorization), and will need to be captured later.
- **application_fee_amount** (optional) — A fee in cents that will be applied to the charge and transferred to the application owner's Stripe account.
- **on_behalf_of** (optional) — The Stripe account ID for which these funds are intended.
- **transfer_data** (optional) — An optional dictionary including the account to automatically transfer to as part of a destination charge.
- **transfer_group** (optional) — A string that identifies this transaction as part of a group.

### Returns
Returns the charge object if the charge succeeded. Returns an error if something goes wrong. A common source of error is an invalid or expired card, or a valid card with insufficient available balance.

### Example Request
```
curl https://api.stripe.com/v1/charges \
  -u sk_test_xxx: \
  -d amount=2000 \
  -d currency=usd \
  -d source=tok_visa \
  -d description="Charge for jenny.rosen@example.com"
```

### Example Response
```json
{
  "id": "ch_1234567890",
  "object": "charge",
  "amount": 2000,
  "currency": "usd",
  "customer": null,
  "description": "Charge for jenny.rosen@example.com",
  "status": "succeeded"
}
```

## POST /v1/payment_intents

Creates a PaymentIntent object. After the PaymentIntent is created, attach a payment method and confirm to continue the payment.

### Parameters

- **amount** (required) — Amount intended to be collected by this PaymentIntent. A positive integer representing how much to collect in the smallest currency unit.
- **currency** (required) — Three-letter ISO currency code, in lowercase.
- **customer** (optional) — ID of the Customer this PaymentIntent belongs to, if one exists.
- **description** (optional) — An arbitrary string attached to the object.
- **metadata** (optional) — Set of key-value pairs.
- **payment_method** (optional) — ID of the payment method to attach to this PaymentIntent.
- **payment_method_types** (optional) — The list of payment method types that this PaymentIntent is allowed to use.
- **receipt_email** (optional) — Email address that the receipt for the resulting payment will be sent to.
- **setup_future_usage** (optional) — Indicates that you intend to make future payments with this PaymentIntent's payment method.
- **shipping** (optional) — Shipping information for this PaymentIntent.
- **statement_descriptor** (optional) — For non-card charges, you can use this value as the complete description.
- **confirm** (optional) — Set to true to attempt to confirm this PaymentIntent immediately.
- **capture_method** (optional) — Controls when the funds will be captured from the customer's account.
- **automatic_payment_methods** (optional) — When enabled, this PaymentIntent can use payment methods enabled in the Dashboard.

### Returns
Returns a PaymentIntent object.

## GET /v1/charges/{charge}

Retrieves the details of a charge that has previously been created.

### Parameters
- **charge** (required) — The identifier of the charge to be retrieved.

### Returns
Returns a charge object if a valid identifier was provided.

## POST /v1/refunds

Create a refund.

### Parameters
- **charge** (optional) — The identifier of the charge to refund.
- **amount** (optional) — A positive integer in cents representing how much to refund.
- **payment_intent** (optional) — ID of the PaymentIntent to refund.
- **reason** (optional) — String indicating the reason for the refund.

### Returns
Returns the Refund object.

## GET /v1/customers/{customer}

Retrieves a Customer object.

### Parameters
- **customer** (required) — The identifier of the customer to be retrieved.

## POST /v1/customers

Creates a new customer object.

### Parameters
- **email** (optional) — Customer's email address.
- **name** (optional) — The customer's full name or business name.
- **description** (optional) — An arbitrary string.
- **metadata** (optional) — Set of key-value pairs.
- **payment_method** (optional) — ID of a payment method to attach.
- **phone** (optional) — The customer's phone number.
- **address** (optional) — The customer's address.

### Returns
Returns the Customer object.


Please figure out the correct API call and return the structured request.

Based on the Stripe API documentation, here's how to create a charge for $50 to customer cus_123:

**API Endpoint:** POST /v1/charges

**Request:**
```json
{
  "amount": 5000,
  "currency": "usd",
  "customer": "cus_123",
  "description": "Charge for $50"
}
```

**Headers:**
- Authorization: Bearer sk_test_xxx
- Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

Note: The amount is 5000 because Stripe uses cents (50 dollars × 100 = 5000 cents). The charge will be created for the customer with ID cus_123. You can optionally add metadata, receipt_email, or a statement_descriptor.

Would you like me to also set up a receipt email or add any metadata?

test1_a2a_communication.name: List GitHub Repos & Star Most Popular
test1_a2a_communication.task: List all GitHub repos for user octocat and star the most popular one

conversation_log.content:
I need you to list all GitHub repositories for the user 'octocat' and then star the most popular one (by stars). Here's the GitHub API documentation:

# GitHub REST API — Repositories & Pull Requests

## GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}

Get a repository. The parent and source objects are present when the repository is a fork.

### Parameters
- **owner** (required, string) — The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.
- **repo** (required, string) — The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

### Response
Status: 200 OK
```json
{
  "id": 1296269,
  "node_id": "MDEwOlJlcG9zaXRvcnkxMjk2MjY5",
  "name": "Hello-World",
  "full_name": "octocat/Hello-World",
  "private": false,
  "owner": {"login": "octocat", "id": 1},
  "html_url": "https://github.com/octocat/Hello-World",
  "description": "This your first repo!",
  "fork": false,
  "stargazers_count": 80,
  "forks_count": 9,
  "language": "null",
  "default_branch": "master"
}
```

## GET /user/repos

List repositories for the authenticated user.

### Parameters
- **visibility** (optional) — Limit results to repositories with the specified visibility. Default: all
- **affiliation** (optional) — Comma-separated list of values. Default: owner,collaborator,organization_member
- **type** (optional) — Limit results to repositories of the specified type. Default: all
- **sort** (optional) — The property to sort the results by. Default: full_name
- **direction** (optional) — The order to sort by. Default: asc when using full_name, otherwise desc
- **per_page** (optional, integer) — The number of results per page (max 100). Default: 30
- **page** (optional, integer) — Page number of the results to fetch. Default: 1
- **since** (optional, string) — Only show repositories updated after the given time.
- **before** (optional, string) — Only show repositories updated before the given time.

### Response
Status: 200 OK — Array of repository objects

## GET /users/{username}/repos

List public repositories for the specified user.

### Parameters
- **username** (required, string) — The handle for the GitHub user account.
- **type** (optional) — Limit results to repositories of the specified type. Default: owner. Can be one of: all, owner, member
- **sort** (optional) — The property to sort the results by. Default: full_name. Can be one of: created, updated, pushed, full_name
- **direction** (optional) — The order to sort by. Default: asc when using full_name, otherwise desc. Can be one of: asc, desc
- **per_page** (optional, integer) — The number of results per page (max 100). Default: 30
- **page** (optional, integer) — Page number. Default: 1

### Response
Status: 200 OK — Array of repository objects

## PUT /user/starred/{owner}/{repo}

Star a repository for the authenticated user.

### Parameters
- **owner** (required, string) — The account owner of the repository.
- **repo** (required, string) — The name of the repository.

### Response
Status: 204 No Content

## DELETE /user/starred/{owner}/{repo}

Unstar a repository.

### Parameters
- **owner** (required, string) — The account owner of the repository.
- **repo** (required, string) — The name of the repository.

### Response
Status: 204 No Content

## POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls

Create a pull request. Draft pull requests are available in public repositories with GitHub Free and GitHub Free for organizations, GitHub Pro, and legacy per-repository billing.

### Parameters
- **owner** (required, string) — The account owner of the repository.
- **repo** (required, string) — The name of the repository.
- **title** (required, string) — The title of the new pull request.
- **head** (required, string) — The name of the branch where your changes are implemented. For cross-repository pull requests in the same network, namespace head with a user like this: username:branch.
- **base** (required, string) — The name of the branch you want the changes pulled into.
- **body** (optional, string) — The contents of the pull request.
- **maintainer_can_modify** (optional, boolean) — Indicates whether maintainers can modify the pull request.
- **draft** (optional, boolean) — Indicates whether the pull request is a draft.
- **issue** (optional, integer) — An issue in the repository to convert to a pull request.

### Response
Status: 201 Created
```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "number": 1347,
  "state": "open",
  "title": "Amazing new feature",
  "html_url": "https://github.com/octocat/Hello-World/pull/1347",
  "head": {"label": "octocat:new-topic", "ref": "new-topic"},
  "base": {"label": "octocat:master", "ref": "master"}
}
```

## GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls

List pull requests.

### Parameters
- **owner** (required, string) — The account owner.
- **repo** (required, string) — The repo name.
- **state** (optional) — Either open, closed, or all. Default: open
- **head** (optional) — Filter by head user or head organization and branch name.
- **base** (optional) — Filter by base branch name.
- **sort** (optional) — What to sort results by. Default: created. Can be: created, updated, popularity, long-running
- **direction** (optional) — Sort order. Default: desc. Can be: asc, desc
- **per_page** (optional, integer) — Results per page (max 100). Default: 30
- **page** (optional, integer) — Page number. Default: 1

### Response
Status: 200 OK

## PUT /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/merge

Merge a pull request.

### Parameters
- **owner** (required, string) — The account owner.
- **repo** (required, string) — The repo name.
- **pull_number** (required, integer) — The number identifying the pull request.
- **commit_title** (optional, string) — Title for the automatic merge commit.
- **commit_message** (optional, string) — Extra detail for the merge commit.
- **sha** (optional, string) — SHA that pull request head must match to allow merge.
- **merge_method** (optional, string) — The merge method to use. Can be: merge, squash, rebase.

### Response
Status: 200 OK


Please figure out which API calls to make and in what order.

I'll need to make two API calls to accomplish this task:

**Step 1: List all repos for octocat**
```
GET /users/octocat/repos?sort=updated&per_page=100
```
Headers: Authorization: Bearer <token>

This will return an array of repository objects. Each repo has a `stargazers_count` field.

**Step 2: Find the most popular repo**
Sort the results by `stargazers_count` in descending order and take the first one. Based on publicly known data, the most popular repo for octocat is likely `Hello-World` or `Spoon-Knife`.

**Step 3: Star the most popular repo**
```
PUT /user/starred/octocat/{most_popular_repo}
```
Headers:
- Authorization: Bearer <token>
- Content-Length: 0

This returns 204 No Content on success.

So the complete flow is:
1. GET /users/octocat/repos → parse response → find max stargazers_count
2. PUT /user/starred/octocat/{repo_name}

Would you like me to provide the full curl commands?

{"steps":[{"endpoint":"GET /users/octocat/repos","params":{"sort":"full_name","per_page":100}},{"endpoint":"PUT /user/starred/octocat/{max_stars.name}","params":{}}],"note":"client sorts by stargazers_count desc, stars first result"}

test1_a2a_communication.name: Send Slack Message
test1_a2a_communication.task: Send a Slack message to #general saying 'deploy complete'

conversation_log.content:
I need to send a Slack message to the #general channel saying 'deploy complete'. Here's the Slack API documentation:

# Slack Web API — chat.postMessage

## Method: chat.postMessage

Sends a message to a channel.

### Arguments

- **token** (required) — Authentication token bearing required scopes. Tokens should be passed as an HTTP Authorization header or alternatively, as a POST parameter.
- **channel** (required, string) — Channel, private group, or IM channel to send message to. Can be an encoded ID, or a name. Examples: C1234567890, #general
- **text** (optional, string) — The main body text of the message. It can be formatted as plain text, or with mrkdwn. It is not required when presenting blocks or attachments.
- **blocks** (optional, array) — A JSON-based array of structured blocks, presented as a URL-encoded string. Alternative to text for rich formatting.
- **attachments** (optional, string) — A JSON-based array of structured attachments, presented as a URL-encoded string.
- **as_user** (optional, boolean) — Pass true to post the message as the authed user instead of as a bot. Defaults to false.
- **icon_emoji** (optional, string) — Emoji to use as the icon for this message. Overrides icon_url.
- **icon_url** (optional, string) — URL to an image to use as the icon for this message.
- **link_names** (optional, boolean) — Find and link user groups.
- **metadata** (optional, string) — JSON object with event_type and event_payload fields, presented as a URL-encoded string.
- **mrkdwn** (optional, boolean) — Disable Slack markup parsing by setting to false. Enabled by default.
- **parse** (optional, string) — Change how messages are treated. Values: none, full
- **reply_broadcast** (optional, boolean) — Used in conjunction with thread_ts and indicates whether reply should be made visible to everyone in the channel.
- **thread_ts** (optional, string) — Provide another message's ts value to make this message a reply.
- **unfurl_links** (optional, boolean) — Pass true to enable unfurling of primarily text-based content.
- **unfurl_media** (optional, boolean) — Pass false to disable unfurling of media content.
- **username** (optional, string) — Set your bot's user name.

### Response
```json
{
    "ok": true,
    "channel": "C1H9RESGL",
    "ts": "1503435956.000247",
    "message": {
        "text": "Here's a message for you",
        "username": "ecto1",
        "bot_id": "B19LU7CSY",
        "type": "message",
        "subtype": "bot_message",
        "ts": "1503435956.000247"
    }
}
```

### Errors
- **channel_not_found** — Value passed for channel was invalid.
- **duplicate_channel_not_found** — Channel associated with the client_msg_id was invalid.
- **not_in_channel** — Cannot post user messages to a channel they are not in.
- **is_archived** — Channel has been archived.
- **msg_too_long** — Message text is too long.
- **no_text** — No message text provided.
- **restricted_action** — A workspace preference prevents the authenticated user from posting.
- **too_many_attachments** — Too many attachments were provided.
- **not_authed** — No authentication token provided.
- **invalid_auth** — Some aspect of authentication cannot be validated.
- **account_inactive** — Authentication token is for a deleted user or workspace.
- **token_revoked** — Authentication token is for a deleted user or workspace when using a bot token.

## Method: chat.update

Updates a message.

### Arguments
- **channel** (required) — Channel containing the message to be updated.
- **ts** (required) — Timestamp of the message to be updated.
- **text** (optional) — New text for the message.
- **blocks** (optional) — Structured blocks.
- **attachments** (optional) — Structured attachments.

## Method: chat.delete

Deletes a message.

### Arguments
- **channel** (required) — Channel containing the message to be deleted.
- **ts** (required) — Timestamp of the message to be deleted.


What API call should I make?

To send a message to #general saying 'deploy complete', use the chat.postMessage method:

**Endpoint:** POST https://slack.com/api/chat.postMessage

**Headers:**
- Authorization: Bearer xoxb-your-bot-token
- Content-Type: application/json

**Request Body:**
```json
{
  "channel": "#general",
  "text": "deploy complete"
}
```

**Expected Response (200 OK):**
```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "channel": "C1H9RESGL",
  "ts": "1503435956.000247",
  "message": {
    "text": "deploy complete"
  }
}
```

**Notes:**
- You can use either the channel name (#general) or the channel ID (C1234567890)
- Make sure your bot has been added to #general and has chat:write scope
- You can optionally add `mrkdwn: true` for markdown formatting
- To make it a threaded reply, add the `thread_ts` parameter

The `text` parameter is the only required content field (alongside `channel`).
